summary.text: Logs the user out by flushing the signer and removing credentials.
Ensure signer is available, creating it from storage if needed
Signs a personal message using the configured signer
Optional parameters to control message signing behavior

Wait for SDK initialization to complete. This triggers async initialization
which includes storage access, so it should only be called in browser environments.

content.text: Promise that resolves when initialization is complete

summary.text: Validates and refreshes the access token if needed.

Ensures async initialization is complete. This is called lazily when
storage is actually needed, not during construction.

content.text: Promise that resolves when async initialization is complete

summary.text:
Ensures the SDK is initialized. This method guarantees that initialization
happens exactly once, even if called concurrently from multiple methods.

content.text: Promise that resolves when initialization is complete

summary.text: Timestamp (in seconds) that specifies the time by which this session MUST expire.
Set of permissions to grant to the user.
Signer to assign the permissions to.
Account to assign the permissions to.
Timestamp (in seconds) that specifies the time by which this session MUST expire.
ERC-4337 Factory to deploy smart contract account.
Calldata to use when calling the ERC-4337 Factory.
Set of policies for the permission. No longer in the 7715 spec but required by viem
Whether or not the wallet must grant the permission.
A URL to send the user to after they are confirmed.
A space-separated list of scopes granted to the OAuth application.
If set to true does not immediately redirect the current browser context to visit the OAuth authorization page for the provider.

readme.text:
Website | Documentation | X

# Openfort.js Library [![Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@openfort/openfort-js.svg)](https://www.npmjs.org/package/@openfort/openfort-js) The Openfort js library provides convenient access to handle client session keys and return signed messages back to Openfort from applications written in client-side JavaScript. ## Installation

## Usage examples
- [Next.js app Embedded Wallet](https://github.com/openfort-xyz/openfort-js/tree/main/examples/apps/auth-sample)
- [Next.js app - Wagmi - Embedded Wallet](https://github.com/openfort-xyz/openfort-js/tree/main/examples/apps/wallet-libraries/next-wagmi)
